question_en: Who co-founded Microsoft with Paul Allen?
context_en: Bill Gates and Paul Allen founded Microsoft in 1975 in Albuquerque. The company moved to Redmond, Washington, in 1979.

question_en: What does the acronym HTTP stand for?
context_en: HTTP was created by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989. HTTPS adds TLS encryption on top of HTTP.

question_en: In what year was the first iPhone released?
context_en: Steve Jobs unveiled the first iPhone on 9 January 2007 and it went on sale on 29 June that year.

question_en: Who is considered the inventor of the World Wide Web?
context_en: Tim Berners-Lee proposed the WWW in 1989 at CERN. The first website (info.cern.ch) is still accessible.

question_en: Which company created the Android operating system?
context_en: Android Inc. was founded in 2003 and bought by Google in 2005. The first public version came out in 2008.

question_en: Which company makes the 'Ryzen' processor?
context_en: AMD launched the Ryzen line in 2017 based on its Zen architecture. It competed head-to-head with Intel after years of the rival's dominance.

question_en: Who founded Tesla and SpaceX?
context_en: Elon Musk co-founded PayPal, SpaceX (2002) and joined as CEO of Tesla in 2008. He also founded Neuralink and The Boring Company.

question_en: How many bits make up a byte?
context_en: A byte has 8 bits. It is the basic unit of information in computing. It allows 256 different values (2^8).

question_en: Which programming language did James Gosling create?
context_en: James Gosling designed Java at Sun Microsystems in 1995. Its slogan: 'Write once, run anywhere' thanks to the JVM.

question_en: Which company created the iOS operating system?
context_en: Apple launched iOS (originally iPhone OS) with the first iPhone in 2007. It is proprietary and exclusive to Apple devices.

question_en: What does CPU stand for?
context_en: CPU = Central Processing Unit. It is the brain of the computer that executes program instructions.

question_en: Who founded Facebook?
context_en: Mark Zuckerberg founded Facebook in 2004 from his room at Harvard, along with Eduardo Saverin and other classmates.

question_en: In what year was Google founded?
context_en: Larry Page and Sergey Brin founded Google in September 1998 in Menlo Park, California. It was initially called 'Backrub'.

question_en: Which protocol is used to send emails?
context_en: SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) sends emails. POP3 and IMAP retrieve them from the server.

question_en: What was the first web browser?
context_en: Tim Berners-Lee created 'WorldWideWeb' in 1990 (later renamed Nexus). Mosaic (1993) popularized inline images.

question_en: Which language did Guido van Rossum create?
context_en: Guido van Rossum created Python in 1991. It is one of the most-used languages, especially in AI, data science and education.

question_en: Which company created the M1 chip for Mac?
context_en: Apple launched the M1 chip in 2020, its first ARM processor for Mac, replacing Intel after 15 years of collaboration.

question_en: What does USB stand for?
context_en: USB was developed in 1996 by a consortium (Intel, Microsoft, IBM, etc.). USB-C (2014) is now the universal connector.

question_en: Which social network was founded by Jack Dorsey?
context_en: Jack Dorsey co-founded Twitter in 2006. The platform was renamed X in 2023 after Elon Musk's purchase.

question_en: Which generation of mobile networks became popular around 2020?
context_en: 5G began commercial deployment in 2019 and became widespread in 2020-2022, promising more speed and lower latency.

question_en: Who invented the practical electric light bulb?
context_en: Thomas Edison patented the practical incandescent light bulb in 1879. He was not the first inventor, but the most commercial.

question_en: Which company created ChatGPT?
context_en: OpenAI launched ChatGPT in November 2022. Microsoft is one of its main investors and partners (Azure).

question_en: Which company makes GeForce GPUs?
context_en: NVIDIA has made the GeForce line since 1999. Its chips dominate the gaming and AI-training market (with its Tesla/H100).

question_en: What does 'Wi-Fi' mean?
context_en: Despite the myth, 'Wi-Fi' does not stand for 'Wireless Fidelity'. It was a brand name created by Interbrand in 1999.

question_en: In what year was Apple founded?
context_en: Apple was founded by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak and Ronald Wayne on 1 April 1976 in California.

question_en: Which cryptocurrency did Satoshi Nakamoto create?
context_en: Bitcoin was created by the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto in 2009. The true identity remains a mystery.

question_en: Which language did Brendan Eich create in 10 days?
context_en: Brendan Eich designed JavaScript in 10 days at Netscape, in 1995. It is the main language of the browser.

question_en: Which company created the Bing search engine?
context_en: Microsoft launched Bing in 2009 as the successor to MSN Search/Live Search. It fights for a small share against Google.

question_en: Which operating system did Linus Torvalds develop?
context_en: Linus Torvalds released the first version of Linux in 1991, while studying at the University of Helsinki.

question_en: How many bytes are in a kilobyte (KB) according to the SI?
context_en: According to the SI, 1 KB = 1,000 bytes. In computing, 1 KiB = 1,024 bytes is used. There is historical confusion in usage.

question_en: Which company created the iPad?
context_en: Apple launched the first iPad in April 2010. It defined the modern tablet format.

question_en: Which platform did Microsoft buy in 2016 for $26 billion?
context_en: Microsoft bought LinkedIn for $26.2 billion in 2016. In 2018 it bought GitHub for $7.5 billion.

question_en: What type of storage are SSDs?
context_en: SSDs (Solid State Drives) use NAND flash memory. They are much faster than HDDs (mechanical disks).

question_en: Which company created Photoshop?
context_en: Adobe launched Photoshop in 1990. It is the industry standard for raster image editing.

question_en: Who founded Amazon?
context_en: Jeff Bezos founded Amazon in 1994 in Seattle, initially as an online bookstore. He handed the CEO role to Andy Jassy in 2021.

question_en: What technology does blockchain use?
context_en: Blockchain is a distributed ledger of cryptographically linked blocks. It is the basis of Bitcoin and many cryptocurrencies.

question_en: Which company created WhatsApp?
context_en: WhatsApp Inc. was founded in 2009 by Jan Koum and Brian Acton. Facebook (now Meta) bought it in 2014 for $19 billion.

question_en: In what year was Windows 95 released?
context_en: Microsoft released Windows 95 on 24 August 1995. It introduced the Start menu and revolutionized the desktop.

question_en: Which company created the DuckDuckGo search engine?
context_en: DuckDuckGo was founded by Gabriel Weinberg in 2008. It stands out for not tracking users' searches.

question_en: Which protocol does most modern websites use?
context_en: HTTPS (HTTP over TLS) encrypts traffic. Since 2018 most websites use it; browsers warn if a site is HTTP-only.

question_en: Which company created the Swift language?
context_en: Apple introduced Swift in 2014, at WWDC. It progressively replaced Objective-C in iOS/macOS development.

question_en: In what year was YouTube founded?
context_en: YouTube was founded by Chad Hurley, Steve Chen and Jawed Karim in February 2005. Google bought it in 2006 for $1.65 billion.

question_en: What is measured in MHz/GHz in a processor?
context_en: The clock frequency indicates cycles per second (1 GHz = 1 billion). Along with the IPC, it determines performance.

question_en: Which TCP/IP network preceded the modern Internet?
context_en: ARPANET was developed by DARPA (U.S.) from 1969. It connected the first universities and evolved into the Internet in the 1980s.

question_en: Which company created the PlayStation console?
context_en: Sony launched the first PlayStation in 1994. It was originally a joint project with Nintendo that fell apart.

question_en: Which company tried to acquire ARM in 2020 (deal ultimately canceled)?
context_en: NVIDIA announced in 2020 the purchase of ARM for $40 billion. The deal was canceled in 2022 over regulatory hurdles.

question_en: Which language did Dennis Ritchie create in the 70s?
context_en: Dennis Ritchie created C at Bell Labs in 1972. It is one of the most influential languages; the parent of C++, C#, Objective-C, etc.

question_en: Which company created Slack?
context_en: Slack was founded by Stewart Butterfield in 2013. Salesforce bought it in 2021 for $27.7 billion.

question_en: Which project aimed to map all human genes?
context_en: The Human Genome Project (1990-2003) sequenced the ~3 billion base pairs of human DNA.

question_en: Which company created the Unreal Engine graphics engine?
context_en: Epic Games has developed Unreal Engine since 1998. Version 5 (2022) leads the industry in graphical realism.

question_en: Which company created the Snapdragon chip?
context_en: Qualcomm makes the Snapdragon chips, present in many Android smartphones.

question_en: Which company created the Core i7 processor?
context_en: Intel launched the Core i7 processors in 2008 as its high-end range for PCs.

question_en: What does 'RAM' stand for?
context_en: RAM stands for 'Random Access Memory'. It stores data temporarily while the device is on.

question_en: What does 'GPU' stand for?
context_en: GPU stands for 'Graphics Processing Unit'. A processor specialized in graphics calculations and, today, AI.

question_en: Which technology enables OLED screens?
context_en: OLEDs are organic light-emitting diodes. Each pixel emits its own light, without needing a backlight.

question_en: Which company created the Steam video game platform?
context_en: Valve created Steam in 2003. It is the largest video game distribution platform for PC.

question_en: Who created Linux?
context_en: Linus Torvalds created the Linux kernel in 1991, as a student in Helsinki. He distributed it as free software.

question_en: What is GNU?
context_en: GNU is a free-software project started by Richard Stallman in 1983. It inspired the free-software movement.

question_en: Which company created the Go language?
context_en: Google created the Go language in 2009. Designed by Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike and Ken Thompson.

question_en: Which company created the Kotlin language?
context_en: Kotlin was created by JetBrains in 2011. Google adopted it as the preferred language for Android in 2019.

question_en: Which company created the C# language?
context_en: Microsoft created C# (C Sharp) in 2000 as part of the .NET platform, led by Anders Hejlsberg.

question_en: Which company created the Java language?
context_en: Sun Microsystems created Java in 1995. Sun was acquired by Oracle in 2010.

question_en: Which company acquired Sun Microsystems?
context_en: Oracle bought Sun Microsystems in 2010 for about $7.4 billion.

question_en: Which social network did Facebook (Meta) buy in 2012?
context_en: Facebook (now Meta) bought Instagram in 2012 for $1 billion.

question_en: Which company bought Twitter in 2022?
context_en: Elon Musk completed the purchase of Twitter in October 2022 for about $44 billion. He renamed it 'X' in 2023.

question_en: What has Twitter been called since 2023?
context_en: Elon Musk renamed Twitter to 'X' in July 2023.

question_en: In what year was Facebook founded?
context_en: Facebook was founded in February 2004 by Mark Zuckerberg and other Harvard students.

question_en: What has Facebook been officially called as a company since 2021?
context_en: Facebook Inc. changed its corporate name to Meta Platforms in October 2021.

question_en: What has Google's parent company been called since 2015?
context_en: Google reorganized under Alphabet Inc. in 2015. Sundar Pichai is CEO of Google and Alphabet.

question_en: Who is the CEO of Microsoft (as of 2024)?
context_en: Satya Nadella has been CEO of Microsoft since February 2014.

question_en: Who was the first CEO of Apple after Steve Jobs's death?
context_en: Tim Cook succeeded Steve Jobs as CEO of Apple in August 2011.

question_en: In what year did Steve Jobs die?
context_en: Steve Jobs died on 5 October 2011 at age 56, due to pancreatic cancer.

question_en: Which company created the Xbox console?
context_en: Microsoft launched the first Xbox in 2001. It currently sells the Xbox Series X/S (2020).

question_en: Which company created the Nintendo Switch console?
context_en: Nintendo launched the Switch in March 2017. A hybrid between home console and handheld.

question_en: Which company created the game Minecraft?
context_en: Markus Persson and his company Mojang created Minecraft. Microsoft bought Mojang in 2014 for $2.5 billion.

question_en: Which company created Fortnite?
context_en: Epic Games developed Fortnite, released in 2017. It is one of the most popular video games in history.

question_en: Which company created the Unity engine?
context_en: Unity Technologies (Denmark) created the Unity engine, released in 2005. Very popular for mobile games.

question_en: Which company created GTA?
context_en: Rockstar Games has developed the 'Grand Theft Auto' saga since 1997.

question_en: Who is the owner of OpenAI?
context_en: OpenAI is a private company in which Microsoft has invested more than $13 billion. Sam Altman is its CEO.

question_en: Who is the CEO of OpenAI (as of 2024)?
context_en: Sam Altman is the CEO of OpenAI. He was briefly ousted in November 2023 and reinstated a few days later.

question_en: What type of AI is ChatGPT?
context_en: ChatGPT is a generative AI based on GPT LLMs (Large Language Models) developed by OpenAI.

question_en: What does 'LLM' stand for?
context_en: LLM stands for Large Language Model: a language model trained on large amounts of text.

question_en: Which company created Gemini (formerly Bard)?
context_en: Google created Gemini (renamed from Bard in 2024), its conversational AI assistant.

question_en: Which company created Claude?
context_en: Anthropic, founded by former OpenAI employees, created the Claude family of models.

question_en: Which company created LLaMA?
context_en: Meta launched LLaMA in 2023, its open language models for research.

question_en: How many GB are in a terabyte?
context_en: In decimal notation (SI), 1 TB = 1,000 GB. In binary (IEC), 1 TiB = 1,024 GiB.

question_en: How many bytes are in a megabyte (SI)?
context_en: According to the SI, 1 MB = 1,000,000 bytes. In binary (MiB), 1 MiB = 1,048,576 bytes.

question_en: Which protocol is used to transfer files?
context_en: FTP (File Transfer Protocol) is the classic protocol for file transfer. Today SFTP is used more.

question_en: Which protocol is used for secure email?
context_en: POP3S, SMTPS and IMAPS are the secure versions (with TLS) of the mail protocols.

question_en: What does DNS stand for?
context_en: DNS (Domain Name System) translates domain names (like example.com) into IP addresses.

question_en: Which company created the PlayStation 5 console?
context_en: Sony launched the PlayStation 5 in November 2020.

question_en: Which company owns YouTube?
context_en: Google bought YouTube in 2006 for $1.65 billion in shares.

question_en: When did Microsoft release Windows 11?
context_en: Microsoft released Windows 11 on 5 October 2021.

question_en: How many bits does the x64 architecture have?
context_en: The x64 architecture (also AMD64 or x86-64) is 64-bit.

question_en: Which company created the GeForce RTX GPU?
context_en: NVIDIA launched the GeForce RTX family in 2018, with the first Turing architecture.

question_en: Which company created the Radeon GPUs?
context_en: ATI launched the Radeon line in 2000. AMD bought ATI in 2006 and keeps the brand.

question_en: How many pins does a Type A HDMI connector have?
context_en: A Type A HDMI connector (standard) has 19 pins.

question_en: In what year was USB-C launched?
context_en: The USB-C standard was finalized in August 2014.

question_en: Which company originally created Photoshop?
context_en: Thomas and John Knoll created Photoshop. Adobe licensed it in 1988 and bought the code in 1995.

question_en: Which company created the PDF format?
context_en: Adobe created the PDF format (Portable Document Format) in 1993. It has been an ISO standard since 2008.

question_en: Who is Tim Berners-Lee?
context_en: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989 while working at CERN.

question_en: At which institution did Berners-Lee work when he invented the web?
context_en: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN (Switzerland) in 1989. The first website was published in 1991.

question_en: Which language does the web use to structure content?
context_en: HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the markup language that structures web pages.

question_en: Which language styles web pages?
context_en: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) describes the presentation of an HTML page.

question_en: Which language adds interactivity to web pages?
context_en: JavaScript, created by Brendan Eich in 1995, is the main programming language of the web.

question_en: Which JavaScript framework did Facebook create?
context_en: Facebook (now Meta) created React in 2013, a JavaScript library for building user interfaces.

question_en: Which JavaScript framework did Google create?
context_en: Google created Angular (originally AngularJS in 2010, and a rewritten Angular in 2016).

question_en: Who created Node.js?
context_en: Ryan Dahl created Node.js in 2009. It allows running JavaScript outside the browser, on the server.

question_en: Which company created TypeScript?
context_en: Microsoft created TypeScript in 2012. It is a superset of JavaScript with static typing.

question_en: Which relational database does Oracle manage?
context_en: Oracle Database is the main DBMS of the Oracle company.

question_en: Which relational database is open source and popular in web applications?
context_en: MySQL is one of the most popular DBMSs in web applications. It has been owned by Oracle since 2010.

question_en: What type of database is MongoDB?
context_en: MongoDB is a NoSQL database oriented to JSON (BSON) documents.

question_en: Which company created Kubernetes?
context_en: Google created Kubernetes (K8s) and released it as open source in 2014. Now it is maintained by the CNCF.

question_en: Which company created Docker?
context_en: Docker Inc. (formerly dotCloud) created Docker in 2013, popularizing containerization.

question_en: Which company created Android?
context_en: Android Inc. founded the platform in 2003. Google bought Android Inc. in 2005 and launched the system in 2008.

question_en: Which company created iOS?
context_en: Apple created iOS (originally 'iPhone OS') in 2007, along with the first iPhone.

question_en: Which company created macOS?
context_en: Apple created macOS (formerly Mac OS X), based in part on the NeXTSTEP system that Apple acquired in 1997.

question_en: Which company created Windows?
context_en: Microsoft launched Windows 1.0 in 1985. It is the most-used PC operating system in the world.

question_en: In what year was Windows XP released?
context_en: Microsoft released Windows XP in October 2001. It is one of the most successful operating systems in its history.

question_en: What type of memory is EEPROM?
context_en: EEPROM (Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory) is a non-volatile memory that can be rewritten.

question_en: What type of memory is DRAM?
context_en: DRAM (Dynamic RAM) is the most common RAM in computers. It is constantly refreshed.

question_en: Who is Sundar Pichai?
context_en: Sundar Pichai has been CEO of Google since 2015 and of Alphabet since 2019.

question_en: Who is Mark Zuckerberg?
context_en: Mark Zuckerberg co-founded Facebook (now Meta) in 2004 at Harvard.

question_en: Who is Sergey Brin?
context_en: Sergey Brin co-founded Google with Larry Page in 1998.

question_en: Who is Larry Page?
context_en: Larry Page co-founded Google with Sergey Brin. Both were doctoral students at Stanford.

question_en: Which company created the TikTok app?
context_en: ByteDance, a Chinese company, created TikTok (Douyin in China) in 2016.

question_en: In what year was TikTok launched internationally?
context_en: TikTok was launched internationally in 2017, merging with Musical.ly in 2018.

question_en: Who created WhatsApp?
context_en: Jan Koum and Brian Acton created WhatsApp in 2009. Facebook bought it in 2014.

question_en: Who created Dropbox?
context_en: Drew Houston and Arash Ferdowsi founded Dropbox in 2007.

question_en: Who created Spotify?
context_en: Daniel Ek and Martin Lorentzon founded Spotify in Sweden in 2006. Public launch in 2008.

question_en: Who founded Netflix?
context_en: Reed Hastings and Marc Randolph founded Netflix in 1997, initially as a DVD-by-mail rental service.

question_en: In what year did Netflix start producing original series?
context_en: Netflix started with originals in 2013, with 'House of Cards' and 'Orange Is the New Black'.

question_en: The search engine Yandex was created by a company from which country?
context_en: Yandex is the Russian search and technology company, founded in 1997. The equivalent of Google in Russia.

question_en: Baidu was created by a company from which country?
context_en: Baidu is the main Chinese search engine, founded in 2000 by Robin Li and Eric Xu.

question_en: How many colors can a pixel display with 24 bits?
context_en: With 24 bits (8 per RGB channel), 2²⁴ = 16,777,216 different colors can be represented.

question_en: Which company created the Apple Pay payment system?
context_en: Apple launched Apple Pay in 2014, first in the U.S., as a contactless payment system from the iPhone.

question_en: Which company created Google Pay?
context_en: Google launched Google Pay in 2018, merging Android Pay and Google Wallet.

question_en: What technology does the blockchain use?
context_en: Blockchain uses cryptography and distributed nodes to maintain an immutable and verifiable ledger.

question_en: Which was the first cryptocurrency?
context_en: Bitcoin, created by Satoshi Nakamoto, was launched in 2009.

question_en: In what year was the Bitcoin whitepaper published?
context_en: Satoshi Nakamoto published the Bitcoin whitepaper in October 2008.

question_en: Who created Ethereum?
context_en: Vitalik Buterin proposed Ethereum in 2013 and the network launched in 2015.

question_en: In what year was Ethereum launched?
context_en: Ethereum was launched in July 2015. It introduced smart contracts.

question_en: Which company created Zoom?
context_en: Eric Yuan founded Zoom Video Communications in 2011. It became massively popular during the pandemic in 2020.

question_en: In what year did Zoom become massively popular?
context_en: Zoom became massively popular in 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic.

question_en: What type of encryption does AES use?
context_en: AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) is a symmetric encryption algorithm (the same key to encrypt and decrypt).

question_en: What type of encryption does RSA use?
context_en: RSA is an asymmetric encryption algorithm: it uses public and private keys. The basis of the digital signature.

question_en: In what year was the first Macintosh launched?
context_en: Apple launched the first Macintosh on 24 January 1984, with a famous ad by Ridley Scott during the Super Bowl.

question_en: Which company made the Apple Lisa's processor?
context_en: The Apple Lisa (1983) used the Motorola 68000 processor.

question_en: Who is Jeff Bezos?
context_en: Jeff Bezos founded Amazon in 1994 as an online bookstore. He was CEO until 2021.

question_en: In what year was Amazon founded?
context_en: Jeff Bezos founded Amazon in July 1994 in Seattle, originally under the name 'Cadabra'.

question_en: Who is the CEO of Amazon (as of 2024)?
context_en: Andy Jassy succeeded Jeff Bezos as CEO of Amazon in July 2021.

question_en: Which Amazon service is the world leader in cloud computing?
context_en: Amazon Web Services (AWS), launched in 2006, is the world's leading provider of cloud services.

question_en: Which cloud service does Microsoft have?
context_en: Microsoft Azure, launched in 2010, is the main competitor to AWS.

question_en: How many transistors does a modern processor have (order of magnitude)?
context_en: Modern processors have between 10 and 100 billion transistors (tens of billions).
